text: 5 things to know for April 2: Artemis II launch, Trump’s address, Oil prices, China’s nuclear arsenal, Raw cheese - CNN
fullText: CNN’s 5 Things AM brings you the news you need to know every morning.

text: NASA’s Artemis II launches on historic journey to the moon - The Washington Post
fullText: A decision will be made Thursday about whether it is safe for the crew to leave low Earth orbit and begin the more dangerous journey toward the moon.

text: Iran continues strikes across the Persian Gulf despite Trump’s warning - NPR
fullText: Iran continued to target Gulf countries with ballistic missiles and drones Thursday as the U.S. Embassy in Baghdad issued a security alert warning of attacks by Iran-backed militias.

text: 4.6 magnitude earthquake shakes California, centered in Santa Cruz Mountains - San Francisco Chronicle
fullText: A 4.6 magnitude earthquake struck California Thursday morning, with sharp shaking and alerts waking residents across the San Francisco Bay Area.

text: Trump’s VA killed a home loan program. Vets are now losing their homes because of it - NPR
fullText: Foreclosures on VA loans are at their highest level in a decade. VA has a fix but it is months away and could still leave vets worse off than most other homeowners.

text: Inside the blame game roiling Georgia's GOP Senate primary - Politico
fullText: Republicans are worried their infighting could make it harder to defeat Democratic Sen. Jon Ossoff.

text: Have Trump's tariffs worked? This is where things stand a year after 'Liberation Day' - NPR
fullText: It has been a year since President Trump announced double-digit tariffs on imports from around the world. So far, those levies have not produced the economic boom the president promised.

text: Republicans aren’t rushing to save Trump’s ballroom - Politico
fullText: A judge has challenged Congress to approve Trump’s multimillion-dollar construction project, but Republicans aren’t showing any immediate interest in the subject.

text: Republicans announce plan to end record-long DHS shutdown - Axios
fullText: House Speaker Mike Johnson Johnson on Friday called the Senate bill a "joke."

fullText: It wasn't their No. 1 problem, but it was a problem for going number one.

fullText: Trump used a prime-time address to outline his objectives for the war and discuss the military's progress to date. He also repeated an earlier claim that fighting could end in two to three weeks.

text: Powerful 7.4 magnitude earthquake in Indonesia's Molucca Sea kills 1 person, sets off small tsunami - CBS News
fullText: Indonesia, a vast archipelago of more than 280 million people, sits on major seismic faults and is frequently hit by earthquakes​.

text: CDC pauses dozens of types of lab testing during evaluation and in wake of downsizing - AP News
fullText: The federal government’s disease-tracking agency has paused its diagnostic testing for rabies, monkeypox and a number of other infectious diseases. The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention this week posted a list of more than two dozen types of testing …

text: Anthropic took down thousands of GitHub repos trying to yank its leaked source code — a move the company says was an accident - TechCrunch
fullText: Anthropic executives said it was an accident and retracted the bulk of the takedown notices.
